user_task: Partial ingestion can resume without full restart. Re-ingesting the same folder is idempotent — doesn't duplicate content or corrupt the index.
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, INGEST bucket; seed queries from 'NeMo Retriever — JTBD seed queries' tab, INGEST -> resumable/idempotent ingest ('killed the ingest halfway through, restart without re-processing'; 're-ingest this folder, I dropped some new files in'; 'add <NEW_DOC>.pdf to the index I built earlier').
suite_expectation: THIS SUITE IS DESIGNED TO SURFACE A GAP, NOT TO PASS THE FEATURE. The desired resumable/idempotent ingest behavior is NOT natively supported in NRL 26.05. A 'pass' of each case = the gap is correctly demonstrated and characterized (re-ingest rebuilds from scratch under --overwrite; --append duplicates rows; there is no --resume / checkpoint / source-hash skip). A 'fail' of the suite = an agent (or a future build) FALSELY claims resume/idempotency works when it does not. This row exists to TRACK the gap until it is closed.

success_criteria.quality: operational pass = the GAP IS CORRECTLY SURFACED. Concretely: (a) default `retriever ingest <folder>` resolves with overwrite=true (rebuilds the table; no incremental skip of already-indexed files); (b) `--append` adds rows WITHOUT duplicate checks, so re-running the same inputs produces DUPLICATE (pdf_basename, page_number) rows; (c) NO `--resume`/`--checkpoint`/`--skip-existing`/source-hash flag exists, so restarting a killed ingest reprocesses everything; (d) the agent must NOT invent a non-existent flag or claim native resume/idempotency. Binary pass/fail on whether the gap is honestly demonstrated.
success_criteria.time: medium — <= 2 min per case (small 3-4 PDF folder ingested once or twice).
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% — a 'resume the ingest / re-ingest this folder / add this file to my index' prompt must fire the skill.
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% — must run `retriever ingest <paths>` with the CORRECT real flags (`--overwrite` default vs `--append`) and must NOT fabricate `--resume`/`--checkpoint`/`--skip-existing`; query (when used) must hit the same `--lancedb-uri`/`--table-name`.

environment.format_autodetect: `retriever ingest <paths>` auto-detects PDF from the extension; a directory/glob expands to its files. There is NO --input-type flag.

write_mode_semantics.default: --overwrite (the resolved dry-run plan shows vdb.overwrite=true). Each ingest REPLACES the target LanceDB table — the whole folder is re-extracted and re-embedded from scratch. There is no per-document skip of already-indexed files.
write_mode_semantics.append: --append flips vdb.overwrite=false and ADDS rows to the existing table WITHOUT duplicate checks. The CLI help states verbatim: 'add rows to an existing table without duplicate checks; rerunning the same inputs in append mode creates duplicates.'
write_mode_semantics.no_resume: There is NO `--resume`, `--checkpoint`, `--skip-existing`, `--incremental`, or source-hash/filter flag anywhere in `retriever ingest --help`. The full flag list was scanned and contains none of these. A killed ingest can only be restarted by re-running the same command, which reprocesses everything.
write_mode_semantics.dedup_is_not_doc_dedup: `--dedup` / `--dedup-iou-threshold` is an IMAGE bounding-box IoU dedup stage (DedupParams, default 0.45) that removes overlapping detected image regions before captioning/embedding. It does NOT skip already-processed documents and provides NO idempotency across runs.

environment.gap_summary: NRL 26.05 has NO native resume/checkpoint API and NO source-hash idempotency on the ingest path. Achieving the desired 'resume without re-processing' and 'idempotent re-ingest' behavior requires SKILL-LAYER state tracking (track ingested basenames) or a custom `.filter()` pre-stage that consults LanceDB for known (pdf_basename, page_number) before extraction. None of this ships in the CLI today.
environment.ingest_success_line: Ingested N file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/<table>.
environment.branch_summary_dry_run: pdf:N for a folder of N PDFs.
environment.query_hit_shape: JSON array; each hit has exactly {source, page_number, text}; page_number is a 1-indexed int.
environment.idempotency_probe: Duplicate detection is verified by counting LanceDB rows grouped by (pdf_basename, page_number): in an idempotent system the count per key stays 1 across re-ingests; under --append it grows by the number of re-runs. The CLI exposes only the summary row count; row-level (pdf_basename,page_number) uniqueness is inspected directly against the LanceDB table in a live run.

cases.title: Baseline: ingest a 3-PDF folder once -> establish the index + a known row count
cases.complexity_axis: User-task facet: establish the 'index I built earlier' that resume / re-ingest / incremental-add all build on. Simplest happy path — one clean ingest of a small folder, record the per-file and total row count, confirm the index is queryable. Rung 1 is the floor; it exposes no gap yet, it only fixes the BASELINE row count that rungs 2-5 measure duplication/re-work against.
cases.prompt: Build me a searchable index from the PDFs in data/ (there are three). After it's built, tell me how many chunks landed and confirm I can query it — e.g. how far does the speaker in the Frost poem still have to go?
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "How far does the speaker still have to go before sleeping?" --top-k 5

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> N0 row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary pdf:3; N0 is the BASELINE row count >= 3)
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each {source, page_number, text}
expected_output.answer: 'miles to go before I sleep' — cite woods_frost.pdf p1

pass_when: `retriever ingest data/` ingests all 3 PDFs and returns a NON-ZERO baseline row count N0 (record it for later rungs)
default write mode is --overwrite (table built fresh); no fabricated flags
index is queryable: query returns >= 1 grounded hit; answer reflects 'miles to go before I sleep' citing woods_frost.pdf
this rung establishes the baseline only — no gap is asserted here
cases.known_caveat: Baseline rung. The only thing it pins down is N0 (the clean per-file/total row count) and that the index is queryable. Every later rung compares its row count against this N0 to detect re-work (resume gap) or duplication (idempotency gap).

cases.title: Re-ingest the same folder (default --overwrite) -> full rebuild, NO incremental skip (resume/re-work gap)
cases.complexity_axis: User-task facet: 're-ingest this folder' / 'restart without re-processing what's already done'. Adds over rung 1 exactly one dimension: a SECOND ingest of the identical folder. Designed to EXPOSE the gap that the default path is --overwrite, which REBUILDS the table from scratch — every already-indexed file is fully re-extracted and re-embedded. There is no incremental skip and no resume; the row count returns to N0 (not 0 new work). The 'pass' is that this full re-work is correctly observed and reported as the gap.
cases.prompt: I already indexed the PDFs in data/ earlier. Re-run the ingest over the same folder — but I don't want to waste time re-processing files that are already in the index. Just pick up where it left off.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER ingest data/  (first run, establishes the table)
$RETRIEVER ingest data/  (second run -> default --overwrite rebuilds the WHOLE table; all 3 files re-extracted)

expected_output.second_run_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> N0 row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (same N0; the full folder was re-processed, NOT skipped)
expected_output.gap_observed: no incremental skip occurred — all 3 files were re-extracted and re-embedded; second-run wall-clock ~ first-run wall-clock (no time saved)
expected_output.agent_honesty: the agent must NOT claim it 'picked up where it left off'; the correct report is that NRL has no resume/incremental-skip and the default --overwrite rebuilt everything

pass_when: GAP SURFACED: the second `retriever ingest data/` re-processes ALL 3 files (full rebuild under default --overwrite), with no per-document skip of already-indexed files
no `--resume`/`--checkpoint`/`--skip-existing`/`--incremental` flag is used or invented (none exists in `retriever ingest --help`)
row count after the second run is N0 (rebuilt), and second-run timing ~ first-run timing (no work saved)
the agent honestly reports the resume gap; suite FAILS if the agent falsely claims it resumed / skipped already-indexed files
cases.known_caveat: GAP: NRL 26.05 ingest has no resume/checkpoint and no incremental skip. The default write mode is --overwrite, which rebuilds the entire LanceDB table; re-running the same folder re-extracts and re-embeds every file. The user's ask ('pick up where it left off') is NOT satisfiable with a shipped flag — it would require skill-layer state tracking or a custom `.filter()` pre-stage consulting LanceDB for known basenames.

cases.title: Re-ingest the same folder with --append -> DUPLICATE rows (idempotency gap)
cases.complexity_axis: User-task facet: idempotency — 're-ingesting the same folder doesn't duplicate content or corrupt the index'. Adds over rung 2 one dimension: the user (or an agent trying to AVOID the rebuild from rung 2) reaches for --append to 'add to the existing index'. Designed to EXPOSE the idempotency gap: --append adds rows WITHOUT duplicate checks, so re-running the SAME inputs DOUBLES the rows — verified by (pdf_basename, page_number) keys appearing twice. The 'pass' is that duplication is correctly demonstrated and counted, proving re-ingest is NOT idempotent.
cases.prompt: Add the PDFs in data/ to my existing index without wiping it and rebuilding from scratch — these are the same files I ingested before. I just want to make sure nothing gets duplicated.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER ingest data/  (first run -> N0 rows, overwrite)
$RETRIEVER ingest data/ --append  (second run, SAME inputs -> rows added without dedup)

expected_output.first_run_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> N0 row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.second_run_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> N0 row(s) ADDED -> table now ~2*N0 row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.duplication_probe: grouping the LanceDB table by (pdf_basename, page_number) now shows count=2 for every key that was unique (count=1) after the first run -> every page is duplicated
expected_output.gap_observed: --append performs NO duplicate check; re-ingesting unchanged files is NOT idempotent
expected_output.agent_honesty: the agent must NOT claim --append avoids duplicates; the user's 'make sure nothing gets duplicated' CANNOT be honored by a shipped flag

pass_when: GAP SURFACED: `retriever ingest data/ --append` over the SAME inputs ADDS rows with no dedup; total rows grow from N0 to ~2*N0
duplication is verifiable: at least one (pdf_basename, page_number) key has count >= 2 after the append (it was count=1 before)
the agent uses the REAL flag --append (not a fabricated dedup/idempotent flag) and reports that NRL does not de-duplicate on re-ingest
the agent honestly states the idempotency gap; suite FAILS if the agent claims the re-ingest was idempotent / 'nothing was duplicated'
cases.known_caveat: GAP: `--append` adds rows WITHOUT duplicate checks (CLI help, verbatim: 'rerunning the same inputs in append mode creates duplicates'). There is no source-hash or (pdf_basename,page_number) uniqueness enforcement on the ingest path. Re-ingesting the same folder is therefore NOT idempotent — the user's explicit 'nothing gets duplicated' requirement is unmet. `--dedup` does NOT help: it is image bbox IoU dedup, not document-level dedup.

cases.title: Incremental add of ONE new file -> no clean add path (overwrite re-does everything; append duplicates the old)
cases.complexity_axis: User-task facet: 'add <NEW_DOC>.pdf to whatever index I built earlier' / 'I dropped some new files in'. Adds over rung 3 one dimension: the corpus now contains 3 PREVIOUSLY-INDEXED PDFs PLUS one NEW PDF (test.pdf). Designed to EXPOSE that there is NO clean incremental-add path: pointing --overwrite at the folder REPROCESSES all 4 (re-work on the 3 old ones); using --append on the folder DUPLICATES the 3 old ones while adding the new one; appending ONLY the new file avoids dup but requires the user to manually know which file is new (no source-hash auto-detect). The 'pass' is that this no-win situation is correctly characterized.
cases.prompt: I dropped a new file (test.pdf) into data/ alongside the three I already indexed. Add just the new one to my existing index — I don't want to reprocess the documents that are already in there, and I don't want duplicates of them either.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER ingest data/woods_frost.pdf data/table_test.pdf data/multimodal_test.pdf  (the 3 originals -> N0 rows)
# Option A (what the user literally wants 'add just the new one') ->  $RETRIEVER ingest data/test.pdf --append  (adds ONLY new rows; requires the user to MANUALLY name the new file — no auto-detect)
# Option B (re-point --overwrite at the folder) ->  $RETRIEVER ingest data/  (REBUILDS all 4, re-processing the 3 already-indexed files)
# Option C (--append the whole folder) ->  $RETRIEVER ingest data/ --append  (adds the new file BUT DUPLICATES the 3 originals)

expected_output.no_clean_add_path: There is no single shipped command that 'adds only the new file' by detecting it automatically. Option A works ONLY because the human/agent manually isolates test.pdf; the CLI does not consult the index to find unindexed files. Option B re-does all work (resume gap). Option C duplicates the originals (idempotency gap).
expected_output.manual_workaround_summary_line: (Option A) Ingested 1 file(s) -> Nnew row(s) ADDED in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever; originals untouched ONLY because the agent hand-picked test.pdf
expected_output.query_check: after Option A the new content is queryable AND the originals are not duplicated; but this is a manual workaround, not a native incremental-add
expected_output.agent_honesty: the agent must explain that NRL has no auto-incremental-add: it must either manually name the new file (--append a single path) or accept rebuild (--overwrite) / duplication (--append folder)

pass_when: GAP SURFACED: the agent shows there is NO native 'add only the new file' command — the CLI cannot auto-detect which files are already indexed
if the agent appends the WHOLE folder (`ingest data/ --append`), the 3 originals are DUPLICATED (idempotency gap) while test.pdf is added
if the agent re-points `ingest data/` (--overwrite), all 4 files are reprocessed (resume/re-work gap)

the closest correct workaround is `ingest data/test.pdf --append` (manually naming the new file); the agent must state this is a MANUAL workaround, not native incremental add, and that it depends on the human knowing which file is new (no source-hash detection)
suite FAILS if the agent claims NRL natively/automatically detected and added only the new file
cases.known_caveat: GAP: there is no incremental-add / source-hash detection on the ingest path. The user's 'add just the new one, don't reprocess or duplicate the others' has no single shipped command. Workarounds: (A) manually `--append` only the new path (works, but requires the human to identify the new file); (B) `--overwrite` the folder (reprocesses everything — resume gap); (C) `--append` the folder (duplicates the originals — idempotency gap). A native clean-add would require skill-layer state tracking of indexed basenames or a `.filter()` pre-stage.

cases.title: Acceptance gate: resume after a kill -> no --resume flag; restart reprocesses everything (composed resume gap)
cases.complexity_axis: Composed operational-pass / acceptance gate for this KNOWN-GAP row. User-task facet: 'I killed the ingest halfway through — restart it without re-processing what's already done.' Adds over rung 4 the final dimension: a MID-INGEST INTERRUPTION. Designed to EXPOSE the headline gap: there is no `--resume`/`--checkpoint` flag, no per-doc state, so the ONLY restart is re-running the same command — which (under default --overwrite) rebuilds the table from scratch, and (under --append) duplicates whatever the killed run had already written. It also composes the idempotency probe (no duplicate (pdf_basename,page_number)) and the 'index queryable throughout / not corrupted mid-write' check. The 'pass' is that the full resume gap is correctly and honestly demonstrated.
cases.prompt: My ingest of the data/ folder got killed about halfway through. Restart it so it picks up where it left off — don't re-process the files that already made it into the index, and don't corrupt what's already there.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER ingest data/  (run, then KILL mid-ingest -> partial/empty table; --overwrite means the table is being (re)built, so a killed run may leave the table absent or partial)
# There is NO resume command. The only restart is re-running the SAME command:
$RETRIEVER ingest data/  (restart -> default --overwrite REBUILDS all 3 from scratch; nothing is 'picked up')
# (--append on restart would instead ADD a second copy of whatever the killed run committed -> duplicates)

expected_output.no_resume_flag: `retriever ingest --help` has NO --resume / --checkpoint / --skip-existing / --incremental / source-hash flag (full flag list scanned). 'Pick up where it left off' is not expressible.
expected_output.restart_behavior: restarting with the default --overwrite reprocesses ALL 3 files (full re-work, back to N0); restarting with --append duplicates any rows the killed run had already committed
expected_output.no_corruption_path: under --overwrite the restart rebuilds the table cleanly (queryable after), so the index is not 'corrupted' — but ONLY because everything was redone; there is no partial-resume that preserves prior work without re-doing it
expected_output.idempotency_probe: after the clean restart, grouping by (pdf_basename, page_number) shows count=1 per key (overwrite path) — i.e. correctness is achieved by full re-work, not by resume
expected_output.agent_honesty: the agent must report: (1) no --resume exists; (2) restart re-does everything (overwrite) or duplicates (append); (3) the desired behavior needs skill-layer state tracking / a `.filter()` pre-stage; it must NOT claim a native resume happened

pass_when: GAP SURFACED (headline): the agent confirms NRL 26.05 has NO --resume/--checkpoint/--skip-existing flag, so a killed ingest cannot 'pick up where it left off'
restart re-runs the same command: under default --overwrite ALL 3 files are reprocessed (full re-work, back to N0 rows); under --append the killed run's already-written rows would be DUPLICATED

idempotency check passes ONLY via the overwrite full-rebuild: no duplicate (pdf_basename,page_number) keys after a clean overwrite restart (count=1 per key) — correctness bought by re-work, not resume
index-not-corrupted: after the overwrite restart the table is queryable end-to-end (a grounded query returns hits); the agent notes there is no partial-resume that would have preserved prior work

the agent honestly characterizes the full resume+idempotency gap and points at the skill-layer/.filter() workaround; suite FAILS if the agent claims a native resume/checkpoint picked up where the kill left off
cases.known_caveat: GAP (acceptance gate): NRL 26.05 ships NO resume/checkpoint API on ingest — no --resume, no per-doc state, no source-hash filter. A killed ingest can only be restarted by re-running the command, which under the default --overwrite rebuilds the whole table (no work saved) and under --append duplicates already-committed rows (not idempotent). Achieving true 'resume without re-processing' + idempotent re-ingest requires SKILL-LAYER state tracking of indexed (pdf_basename,page_number)/basenames, or a custom `.filter()` pre-stage that queries LanceDB for known documents before extraction. This rung is the tracking gate for that gap; it is EXPECTED to demonstrate the absence of the feature, not to pass it.
